page: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with two NVIDIA H100 GPUs based on the Hopper architecture, providing a total of 159 GB of VRAM. The host system is powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 52 vCPUs (26 physical cores) running at 2.7 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. It features 468.0 GB of system memory, yielding 9.0 GB per core, and includes 1608 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities across compute and memory subsystems. The server achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 2079.0 and a multi-core score of 17415.0, both falling into the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests show strong results with uncached large-block reads at 214.97 GB/sec and writes at 94.7 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the CPU supports Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). LLM inference benchmarks demonstrate text generation speeds of 300.85 tokens per second for a 135M model and 34.78 tokens per second for a 7B model.

Given the inclusion of dual high-end NVIDIA H100 GPUs and dedicated Intel Xeon cores, this server represents a high-density hardware profile optimized for resource-intensive tasks. The qualitative cost efficiency is centered on high-throughput parallel processing, making it suitable for deep learning, LLM inference, scientific simulations, and advanced data science jobs that require massive GPU acceleration and high memory bandwidth.
description: Google Cloud Platform a3-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for demanding parallel computing workloads. It features two NVIDIA H100 GPUs with 159 GB of total VRAM, paired with 52 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs (26 physical cores) running at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 468.0 GB of RAM and 1608 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results show strong performance, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 17415.0 and large-block memory read bandwidth of 214.97 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512, enhancing matrix and vector calculations. This hardware configuration is highly suited for large language model inference, deep learning training, and high-performance computing tasks.

tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform featuring dual NVIDIA H100 GPUs and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for high-throughput parallel computing workloads.

52 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 468 GB system memory
1608 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage

Optimized for deep learning, AI inference, and scientific computing
